raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1485 strengthens the enforcement of state housing laws by granting the Attorney General the unconditional, statutory right to intervene in any pending third party actions brought against local governments for violating state housing laws.",
    "Current law requires the Attorney General to petition the court in order to represent the interests of the state in any third party lawsuits brought against a local government for violating state housing laws.",
    "Most of California’s state housing laws include what's called a 'private right of action,' which allows third parties to file lawsuits when local governments break housing laws.",
    "A third party must file a lawsuit against a local government first for the Attorney General to get involved under current law, which can take months and is at the discretion of the local judge.",
    "AB 1485 would strengthen the Attorney General’s ability to advance Californians’ access to housing and uphold housing law without unnecessary delay.",
    "California needs to build 2.5 million homes by 2031 to address its housing crisis.",
    "Empowering the Attorney General’s office to enforce state housing laws ensures that cities will be held accountable for building their fair share of housing."
  ]
}
```
